revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
'''Anumi Shrine''' (阿沼美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. According to the {{ill|Engi-shiki Shinnōmeichō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki Shinnōmeichō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed as a {{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964}} under the name {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|qid=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province|1=Iyo Province}} {{ill|Onsen District, Ehime|en|Onsen District, Ehime|qid=Q1515027|lt=Onsen District|1=Onsen District, Ehime}} Anumi Shrine. Currently, there are {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}} in Misake Town and Hirata Town. == Overview == It was the only major shrine among the four of {{ill|Onsen District, Ehime|en|Onsen District, Ehime|qid=Q1515027|lt=Onsen District|1=Onsen District, Ehime}}, but it declined early and its location became unknown. Currently, there are {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}} in Misake Town and Hirata Town, although neither has been definitively confirmed. There is also a manuscript written as “Aji Mi Shrine” with the reading “Ajimi”. One theory suggests that this is a misprint『愛媛県の地名』阿沼美神社（松山市味酒町）項。, while another holds that “Ajimi” comes from 熱水 ({{ill|Atsumi|ja|あつみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsumi|1=Q16276725|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16276725}}), meaning “hot spring.” In the 4th year of the {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} era ({{ill|1833|en|1833|qid=Q7587|lt=1833|1=1833}}), a stone inscription bearing the name “Anumi Miyu” from the Nanbokuchō period was discovered in front of the Mishima Shingū in Hirata Village, and the following year the shrine was renamed Anumi Shrine. However, since Hirata Village belonged not to Onsen District but to {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|en|Wake District, Okayama|qid=Q1155355|lt=Wake District (Ehime Prefecture)|1=Wake District, Okayama}}, doubts were raised, and a dispute arose with the Misake Shrine of Misake Village in Onsen District. Misake Shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era ({{ill|1870|en|1870|qid=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}}), and its name was changed to Anumi Shrine. == Anumi Shrine (Misake Town) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Anumi Shrine (阿沼美神社) |image = File:Anumi-Jinja(Shrine) Matsuyama City.JPG |Location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}} Misake Town 3-1-1 |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord|33|50|45.7|N|132|45|23|E|region:JP|name=Anumi Shrine (Misake Town)|qq=}} |Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|lt=Ooyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|Mimi-no-mikoto|ja|味耳命|lt=Mimi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=味耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinai Shrine|lt_ja=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Meishin Taisha|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}|location = {{ill|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|1=Matsuyama}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.846028|N|132.756389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural Shrine|lt_ja=県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |Foundation = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} 3rd year ({{ill|664|en|664|qid=Q30621|lt=664|1=664}}) |Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Ryuzo|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 6|en|October 6|qid=Q2928|lt=October 6|1=October 6}} |Rituals = Natsu-goshi Ōharae-shiki (Rinowa, {{ill|June 30|en|June 30|qid=Q2657|lt=June 30|1=June 30}})
Misake Tenman Shrine Annual Festival ({{ill|July 25|en|July 25|qid=Q2720|lt=July 25|1=July 25}}) }} [[File:阿沼美神社 (味酒町) 入口.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Entrance]] '''Anumi Shrine''' (阿沼美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Misake Town, {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. Under the old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Former Shrine Rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, and its crest—depicted as “bōorishikaku kirisaku sanmoji” (three characters carved with a slanted cut)—matches the family crest of the {{ill|Kōno family|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno family|1=Kōno clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11554232}}. === Enshrined Deities === ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|lt=Ooyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (Ooyamatsumi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|qid=Q644201|lt=Takakami|1=Kuraokami}} (Takakami no Kami; the character 龗 is derived from that used for a shaman transformed into a dragon) * {{ill|Raijin|en|Raijin|qid=Q12808082|lt=Thunder God|1=Raijin}} (Thunder God) * {{ill|Mimi-no-mikoto|ja|味耳命|lt=Mimi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=味耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Mimi-no-mikoto) – A descendant of the eighth generation of {{ill|Takamitama|ja|高魂命|lt=Takamitama|lt_ja=高御魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125400}}, though his deeds are unclear. He is also regarded as an ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kume family|ja|久米氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume family|1=Kume clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369624}} and of the ancient {{ill|Kumekuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|久味国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumekuni-no-miyatsuko|1=Q21652982|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21652982}}. ; Enshrined Together * {{ill|Omotaru Ayakashikone|ja|オモダル・アヤカシコネ|lt=Omotaru Ayakashikone|lt_ja=面足神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru Ayakashikone|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11152535}} (Omodaru no Kami) * {{ill|Omotaru Ayakashikone|ja|オモダル・アヤカシコネ|lt=Omotaru Ayakashikone|lt_ja=惶根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru Ayakashikone|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11152535}} (Kashikone no Kami) * {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|en|Kamuyaimimi|qid=Q11588756|lt=Kamiyai Mimimino-mikoto|1=Kamuyaimimi}} (Kamiyai Mimimino-mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded by {{ill|Ochi no Morihiro|ja|越智守興|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi no Morihiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}’s reign ({{ill|664|en|664|qid=Q30621|lt=664|1=664}}) and was also venerated as the tutelary deity of the Kume clan, descendants of Mimi-no-mikoto. Originally, it stood atop {{ill|Katsuyama|en|Katsuyama|qid=Q1736804|lt=Katsuyama|1=Katsuyama}}, where the current {{ill|Matsuyama Castle (Iyo)|en|Matsuyama Castle (Iyo)|qid=Q981357|lt=Matsuyama Castle|1=Matsuyama Castle (Iyo)}} now stands, and was also known as “Katsuyama Mishima Daimyōjin.” In the 7th year of the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} era ({{ill|1602|en|1602|qid=Q6727|lt=1602|1=1602}}), when {{ill|Katō Yoshiaki|en|Katō Yoshiaki|qid=Q1371657|lt=Kato Kaho|1=Katō Yoshiaki}} constructed Matsuyama Castle, the shrine was relocated to Misake Village and became known as “Misake Shrine.” Thereafter, it was deeply revered by successive lords of the {{ill|Matsuyama Domain|ja|松山藩|lt=Matsuyama Domain|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Domain|1=Matsuyama Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q64578026}}. At that time, the shrine’s temporary palace was located at Nishinobaba in Hori-no-uchi and at Sugidani on the eastern slope of the castle hill, which were regarded as the rear and front demon gates (裏鬼門・表鬼門) of Matsuyama Castle. In addition, the {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|qid=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} temple {{ill|Eikatsuyama Unshō-ji|ja|江勝山雲祥寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eikatsuyama Unshō-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503330|1=Eikatsuyama Unshō‑-ji Temple}}, situated one block to the south, served as the bettō temple ({{ill|Bettō Shrine|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Bettō-ji|qid=Q11396755}}) for Anumi Shrine until the issuance of the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} Edict in the 4th year of the Meiji era ({{ill|1871|en|1871|qid=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}}). In the 14th year of the {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} era ({{ill|1701|en|1701|qid=Q6814|lt=1701|1=1701}}), the shrine buildings were reconstructed. In the 3rd year of the Meiji era ({{ill|1870|en|1870|qid=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}}), it was designated as a {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} – Anumi Shrine – under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Former Shrine Rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, being Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, and its name was restored to the old “Anumi Shrine.” In July of the 20th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} era ({{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}}), during the {{ill|Matsuyama Air Raid|ja|松山大空襲|lt=Matsuyama Air Raid|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Matsuyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22125136}}, many buildings including the shrine hall were destroyed by fire, and in August of the 32nd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1957|en|1957|qid=Q5311|lt=1957|1=1957}}) a reinforced concrete shrine hall was completed. === Shrine Grounds === * Main Hall * Worship Hall – Inside, the inscription of the shrine name, calligraphed by {{ill|Hisamatsu Sadatake|ja|久松定武|lt=Hisamatsu Sadatake|lt_ja=久松定武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisamatsu Sadatake|1=Sadatake Hisamatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369433}}, a direct descendant of the former lord of the Iyo Matsuyama Domain and ex-Governor of Ehime Prefecture, is displayed. === Associated Shrines === [[File:阿沼美神社 (味酒町) 摂末社.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Associated Shrines on the Shrine Grounds]] {{underline|East Side of the Shrine Grounds|qq=}} (from the Main Hall side) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ** Enshrined Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}} (enshrined together with) {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|lt=Iwanaga-hime|lt_ja=盤長姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8191705}}, {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ooyamazukuri|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|lt_ja=木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1781862}}, and {{ill|Sanuki-kibushi|ja|讃岐忌部氏|lt=Sanuki-kibushi|lt_ja=手置帆負命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki-kibushi|1=Sanuki Inbe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11632550}}. * {{ill|Misake Tenman Shrine|ja|味酒天満神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misake Tenman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503332|1=Misake Tenman Shrine}} ** Enshrined Deities: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (enshrined together with) {{ill|Hira no Kagekiyo|ja|平景清|lt=Hira no Kagekiyo|lt_ja=平景清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hira no Kagekiyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原景清|1=Taira no Kagekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7676361}}. ** Founded by Michizane’s disciple {{ill|Misake Yasuyuki|ja|味酒安行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misake Yasuyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. As the Misake Tenjin Festival (Book Presentation Exhibition), an evening Yoimiya Festival with a dedicated koto performance is held on July 24, and on July 25 the annual festival and a kagura ceremony take place. * {{ill|Katsuyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|勝山八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503334|1=Katsuyama Hachiman Shrine}} ** Enshrined Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Ojin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}}), {{ill|Take-mikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|lt=Take-mikazuchi|lt_ja=武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-mikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1502690}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tashimahime|ja|田心姫命|lt=Tashimahime|lt_ja=田心姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashimahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|1=Tagorihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10932870}}, and {{ill|Mizutsu-hime|ja|湍津姫|lt=Mizutsu-hime|lt_ja=瑞津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizutsu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ|1=Tagitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10932592}}. ** It is recognized as the eighth shrine of the {{ill|Matsuyama Hachiman Shrines|ja|松山八社八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Hachiman Shrines|1=Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11529766}}. * {{ill|Iyo Ebisu Shrine|ja|伊余夷子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503336|1=Iyo Ebisu Shrine}} ** Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1067028}} {{underline|West Side of the Shrine Grounds|qq=}} * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} ** Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama|1=Ukanomitama}} (enshrined together with) {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|en|Kamuo Ichihime|qid=Q11588867|lt=Ōichihime|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} === Major Festivals === [[File:Anumi-Jinja(Shrine)2 Matsuyama City.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Rectangular mikoshi (front) and Chigi mikoshi]] [[File:おみこし.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Octagonal mikoshi]] * {{ill|New Year’s Festival|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year’s Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11546824}} ({{ill|January 1|en|January 1|qid=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}}) * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Ininen Festival|1=Kinensai}} ({{ill|February 17|en|February 17|qid=Q2341|lt=February 17|1=February 17}}) * {{ill|Kisaragi Festival|ja|天神信仰|lt=Kisaragi Festival|lt_ja=如月祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|qid=Q66890725}} ({{ill|February 25|en|February 25|qid=Q2356|lt=February 25|1=February 25}}) * {{ill|Hatsu-uma Festival|ja|初午祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu-uma Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 初午|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q510847}} ({{ill|Old February Morning Day|ja|旧2月上午の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old February Morning Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Shanichi Festival|ja|社日|lt=Shanichi Festival|lt_ja=社日祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shanichi Festival|1=Shanichi Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11019584}} ({{ill|March Morning Day|ja|3月上午の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March Morning Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Ebisu Festival|ja|えびす講|lt=Ebisu Festival|lt_ja=夷子祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|April 20|en|April 20|qid=Q2524|lt=April 20|1=April 20}}) * {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|qid=Q11261910|lt=Natsugoshi Festival|1=Ōharae-shiki}} ({{ill|June 30|en|June 30|qid=Q2657|lt=June 30|1=June 30}}) * {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en|Tenjin Matsuri|qid=Q5371810|lt=Tenjin Festival|1=Tenjin Matsuri}} ({{ill|July 24|en|July 24|qid=Q2721|lt=July 24|1=July 24}}, {{ill|July 25|en|July 25|qid=Q2720|lt=July 25|1=July 25}}) * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11385469}} (Yoimiya Festival on {{ill|October 5|en|October 5|qid=Q2930|lt=October 5|1=October 5}}, Classical Annual Festival on {{ill|October 6|en|October 6|qid=Q2928|lt=October 6|1=October 6}}, and a Kami-go Festival (Later Palace Festival) on {{ill|October 7|en|October 7|qid=Q2926|lt=October 7|1=October 7}}) :: In the Kami-go Festival, three mikoshi are paraded from the shrine in the following order: the thunder god’s rectangular mikoshi (in a golden {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmeizukuri style|1=Shinmei-zukuri}}), Ooyamatsumi’s octagonal mikoshi (in a golden {{ill|Takamikura style|ja|高御座|lt=Takamikura style|lt_ja=高御座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikura style|1=Takamikura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213234}}), and Takakami’s square mikoshi (in a black, continuous‐roof style—the typical square mikoshi). The square and octagonal mikoshi, known as the “fighting mikoshi” ({{ill|Hachiawase|ja|鉢合わせ|lt=Hachiawase|lt_ja=鉢合わせ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiawase|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), are particularly famous. In ancient times, it was said that the octagonal mikoshi was borne by villagers (farmers) and the square by townspeople. :: During the autumn festival season in old Matsuyama City (when many shrines held their Kami-go Festivals on October 7), {{ill|Michiya Mihashi|en|Michiya Mihashi|qid=Q3236273|lt=Michiya Mihashi|1=Michiya Mihashi}}’s {{ill|Mikoshi Ondo|ja|神輿音頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Ondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was played in various locations, with the lyrics recounting the “fighting” of the square and octagonal mikoshi. * {{ill|Kotohira Festival|ja|金刀比羅神社|lt=Kotohira Festival|lt_ja=金刀比羅祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kotohira shrines|qid=Q11646488}} ({{ill|November 10|en|November 10|qid=Q2988|lt=November 10|1=November 10}}) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} ({{ill|November 23|en|November 23|qid=Q3021|lt=November 23|1=November 23}}) * On June 30 during the Natsugoshi Festival and on July 24 during the Misake Tenjin Shrine Yoimiya, a night market (Rinowa Night Market and Tenjin Night Market) is held from the early evening in the adjacent Kayacho Shopping Street, attracting large crowds. === Local Information === ; Location * {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}} Misake Town 3-1-1 ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Iyotetsu|en|Iyotetsu|qid=Q3138970|lt=Iyo Railway|1=Iyotetsu}} {{ill|Takahama Line|en|Takahama Line|qid=Q9026173|lt=Takahama Line|1=Takahama Line}}, {{ill|Jōhoku Line (Iyotetsu)|en|Jōhoku Line (Iyotetsu)|qid=Q8192892|lt=Jōhoku Line|1=Jōhoku Line (Iyotetsu)}}, and {{ill|Ōtemachi Line|en|Ōtemachi Line|qid=Q5836444|lt=Otemachi Line|1=Ōtemachi Line}} (city tram) at {{ill|Furumachi Station|ja|古町駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Komachi Station|qid=Q11411531}} (5-minute walk) * Also on the {{ill|Iyotetsu|en|Iyotetsu|qid=Q3138970|lt=Iyo Railway|1=Iyotetsu}} {{ill|Honmachi Line|en|Honmachi Line|qid=Q9026109|lt=Honmachi Line|1=Honmachi Line}} (city tram) at {{ill|Honmachi 4-chome Stop|ja|本町四丁目停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmachi 4-chome Stop|1=Hommachi 4 Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11520704}} (5-minute walk) ; Nearby Facilities * {{ill|Izumo Taisha Matsuyama Branch Shrine|ja|出雲大社松山分祠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha Matsuyama Branch Shrine|1=Izumo Taisha Matsuyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395866}} * {{ill|Dairinji|ja|大林寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairinji|1=Q11436205|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11436205}} – the family temple of the lord of the {{ill|Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|en|Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|qid=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama Domain|1=Iyo-Matsuyama Domain}} ({{ill|Kusamatsu family|ja|久松氏|lt=Matsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusamatsu family|1=Hisamatsu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369441}}) === External Links === * [http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=7188 Anumi Shrine] (Ehime Prefectural Shrine Office) == Anumi Shrine (Hirata Town) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Anumi Shrine (阿沼美神社) |image = [[File:Hirataanumijinjahonden.JPG|250px|阿沼美神社（平田町）本殿]]
Main Hall |Location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}} Hirata Town, Miyauchi 983 |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord|33|53|18|N|132|45|17|E|region:JP|name=Anumi Shrine (Hirata Town)|qq=}} |Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|lt=Ooyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Meishin Taisha|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Village Shrine|lt_ja=郷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Ryuzo|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 4|en|October 4|qid=Q2929|lt=October 4|1=October 4}} }} [[File:阿沼美神社（平田町）.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] '''Anumi Shrine''' (阿沼美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hirata Town, {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. Under the old Modern Shrine Ranking System it was classified as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, this place was once inhabited by a son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Prince Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, who was the progenitor of the Iyo Mimura clan ({{ill|Takuni no Moromichi|ja|武国凝別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuni no Moromichi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武国凝別皇子|1=Prince Takekunikoriwake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11545861}}), and by a son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|lt=Yamato Takeru|lt_ja=日本武尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}, the progenitor of the Iyo Betsukun; successive Iyo Betsukun deities were enshrined here as Anumi Kami. Later, the {{ill|Kōno family|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno family|1=Kōno clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11554232}} combined the worship of the Great Mountain Deity and renamed the shrine “Anumi Mishima Daimyōjin”, later also calling it “Anumi Mishima Shingū”. However, after the fall of the Kōno family, it became simply known as “Mishima Shingū.” In the 2nd year of the {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|qid=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} era ({{ill|1782|en|1782|qid=Q7740|lt=1782|1=1782}}), a ridge plaque inscribed with “Anumi Mishima Shingū” was discovered in the Main Hall. Furthermore, in the 4th year of the {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} era ({{ill|1833|en|1833|qid=Q7587|lt=1833|1=1833}}), beneath the divine bridge in front of the shrine, a stone inscription carved with “Anumi Miyu” dating to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enbun|en|Enbun|qid=Q1145071|lt=Enbun|1=Enbun}} era ({{ill|1360|en|1360|qid=Q6150|lt=1360|1=1360}}) was unearthed. Consequently, in the following year, the 5th year of Tenpō ({{ill|1834|en|1834|qid=Q7591|lt=1834|1=1834}}), the Yoshida family obtained authorization to designate it as a Shikinai Shrine – Anumi Shrine – and its name was accordingly changed. === Enshrined Deities === * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|lt=Ooyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|en|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q595520|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|qid=Q644201|lt=Takakami|1=Kuraokami}} * {{ill|Raijin|en|Raijin|qid=Q12808082|lt=Thunder God|1=Raijin}} === Associated Shrines === * Combined shrine of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴布祢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴船神社}}, {{ill|Juroku Ōji Shrine|ja|十六皇子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juroku Ōji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503337|1=Juroku Ōji Shrine}}, and {{ill|Shinden Shrine|ja|神殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinden Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503340|1=Shinden Shrine}}. * Combined shrine of {{ill|Anawa Daimyōjin|ja|阿奈波大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anawa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503341|1=Anawa Daimyōjin}}, {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}, and {{ill|Taga Daijingū|ja|多賀大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Daijingū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503343|1=Taga Daijingū}}. === Local Information === ; Location * {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}} Hirata Town, Miyauchi 983 ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|qid=Q496531|lt=JR Shikoku|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} on the {{ill|Yosan Line|en|Yosan Line|qid=Q575016|lt=Yosan Line|1=Yosan Line}} at {{ill|Iyo-Wake Station|en|Iyo-Wake Station|qid=Q899231|lt=Iyo Wake Station|1=Iyo-Wake Station}} (approximately a 20-minute walk) === External Links === * [http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=5533 Anumi Shrine] (Ehime Prefectural Shrine Office) == Footnotes == {{reflist|qq=}} == References == * 『日本歴史地名大系 愛媛県の地名』 ({{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}) – entries for Anumi Shrine (Misake Town, Matsuyama) and Anumi Shrine (Hirata Town, Matsuyama) {{Shinto Yok|qq=}} {{Iwanaga Faith|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|阿沼美神社|version=104369789|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Anumi Shrine (Q11657487)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iyo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !!
